metadata.title: FeatureIssue — GitHub feature issue field contract

description: Field contract for GitHub Feature issues in the CORE Roadmap project. The GitHub issue template at .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature.yml implements this contract. When the template and this contract diverge, this contract is authoritative. Authority: ADR-065 (documentation layer separation). Feature issues are the join point between the feature registry (.specs/papers/CORE-Features.md), the roadmap (GitHub CORE Roadmap project), and governing specs/ADRs. The feature_id field is the primary join key.

feature_id.description: Canonical identifier from .specs/papers/CORE-Features.md. Primary join key. Must match a declared F-ID; a feature issue for an undeclared F-ID is invalid until the registry is updated.

status.description: Implementation state. shipping = active in CORE Solo; partial = foundational work exists, not yet complete; roadmap = designed, not yet implemented. Must be kept in sync with CORE-Features.md; CORE-Features.md is authoritative when they diverge.

scope.description: Artifact-type scope. primitive = artifact-agnostic governance engine capability; source-code instantiation = current implementation scoped to source code; extension = extends the engine to new artifact types.

introduced_in_tier.description: First tier this feature appears in. All higher tiers inherit it. tier_availability is declared explicitly only where the inheritance rule is overridden.

tier_availability.description: Tiers that include this feature. Normally derived from introduced_in_tier. Declared explicitly only where inheritance is broken.

description.description: What this feature is and does. Must be consistent with the authoritative description in CORE-Features.md. Artifact-agnostic where scope is primitive.

why_it_matters.description: Governance or commercial significance at its tier. Written for a tier-appropriate reader. 1-3 sentences.

blocked_by.description: F-IDs this feature depends on. Informational — canonical dependency record is the GitHub blocking relationship on the issue. Both must be kept in sync.

blocks.description: F-IDs that depend on this feature. Same sync requirement as blocked_by.

spec_ref.description: Canonical anchor in the feature registry. Format: .specs/papers/CORE-Features.md#F-XX.

intent_ref.description: Governing .intent/ file(s), if any. Free-form path or comma-separated paths.
